zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Driby於大約1254年出生在英國越可爾衛克菲德時，他的父親Simon de Driby29歲，母親Alice Fitzhugh Greasley17歲。他和Joan Tattershall至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1321年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Driby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Riby (Lincs) or perhaps Ribby (Lancs); on the latter name,  see also  Rigby  . See also   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames  .

variants.name: Rigby
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Ribby in Lancashire, named with Old Scandanavian hryggr ‘ridge’ + bȳ ‘farmstead, village’. Compare Rigsby .

variants.name: Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames
meanings: English: locative name, probably from Gilderdale (Forest) in Alston (Cumb), as suggested by   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames  , rather than from Gildersdale in Warter (ER Yorks), as suggested by   Reaney and Wilson  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Driby was born about 1254, in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England, his father, Simon de Driby, was 29 and his mother, Alice Fitzhugh Greasley, was 17. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Joan Tattershall. He died in 1321, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Joan Tattershall. Il est décédé en 1321, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.
